summary: High‑energy 1971 show captured on a soundboard with a classic Dark Star → Wharf Rat → Dark Star jam; slight high‑pitched hiss but overall solid audio.
review: The performance is a fan favorite, highlighted by the smoking Dark Star/Wharf Rat/Dark Star sequence that earned its own “Beautiful Jam” title.  While the soundboard source is generally clear, a faint high‑frequency whine from the master reels can be heard, though most listeners find it tolerable and prefer the un‑reduced version.  Overall the show radiates high energy with standout moments from Dark Star, Greatest Story Ever Told, Bertha, Sugar Magnolia and Candyman.

ai_rating.rationale: The overwhelming majority of reviews are 4‑5 stars praising the music; only a single low rating notes a technical issue, so the combined show (70%) and recording (30%) score is very high.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear soundboard mix with occasional high‑pitched interference/hiss present on the master reels; some reviewers suggest noise reduction but most prefer the original despite the whine.

standout_songs: Dark Star > Wharf Rat > Dark Star (Beautiful Jam)
poor_songs: Playing in the Band (called redundant and slow by one reviewer)
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows tightly with rapid song-to-song transitions, especially the Dark Star/Wharf Rat medley that ties the first and second sets together; new material like Greatest Story Ever Told is introduced seamlessly.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for an emotional, searing solo in Dark Star, use of wah‑wah pedal on Sugar Magnolia, and strong vocal presence on several tunes.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned sharing vocals with Bobby on the dark‑star jam; overall solid rhythm work.
band_member_comments.Keys: Ned Lagin’s clavichord, Farfisa organ and electric piano contributions are highlighted during Dark Star, Casey Jones and Johnny B. Goode sections.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey Hart's performance noted as his last before a three‑year hiatus; drumming described as solid throughout.
